metadata.title: Advising Privacy Gate: Anonymize Before Prompting

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Bonjour, Liam — in for Bear. This is Liam, in for Bear? Can you explain it, Bear?

props.command: This is Liam, in for Bear?
props.segment: Advising Privacy Gate: Anonymize Before Prompting

beats.narration_text: The faculty member had an advising appointment in thirty minutes. She pasted the student's name, student ID, transcript excerpt, and disclosed accommodation into Claude and asked for help preparing talking points. It was a reasonable use of Claude for advising preparation. It was also a FERPA disclosure — she had entered educational records into a third-party AI system without a data agreement that authorized it. This is Liam, in for Bear.
beats.new_visual_element: ClaudeComposerAsk — cold open, advising prep with student name and records

props.command: help me prepare for my advising meeting with Maria Chen, who received an incomplete in Chem 301

beats.narration_text: FERPA protects educational records. Entering a student's name, student ID, grades, enrollment status, declared major, or disclosed accommodation into a public AI tool may constitute a disclosure — even if you trust that Claude does not retain the data. The entry itself is the disclosure. The question is not whether Claude is trustworthy. The question is whether your institution has a data agreement with Anthropic that covers this use. Most do not.
beats.new_visual_element: FERPA rule — entry is disclosure / data agreement required / most institutions lack one

beats.narration_text: The workaround is not a lesser approach — it preserves everything useful. Instead of 'my student Maria Chen is a junior who received an incomplete in Chem 301,' use: 'I have an advising appointment with a junior student who received an incomplete in a science course and is considering late withdrawal. Help me think through the conversation.' The situation is preserved. The identity is not disclosed. Claude's help is the same.
beats.new_visual_element: Anonymized composite — situation preserved / identity removed / Claude's help unchanged

beats.narration_text: Before prompting for any advising preparation, check: does this contain the student's name, student ID, grades, enrollment status, declared major, or disclosed accommodation? If yes, replace each with a description. A junior, not Maria Chen. An incomplete in a science course, not Chem 301 grade. A student with an accommodation for extended time, not a named disability. The description preserves the advising context. The identifying information does not need to be there.
beats.new_visual_element: What to remove — name / ID / grades / enrollment / major / accommodation → descriptions

beats.narration_text: The safer habit is to never put identifying student information into a public AI tool — not because Claude is untrustworthy, but because the institutional obligation exists regardless. Advising preparation with anonymized composites is not a lesser use of Claude. It is the correct use: Claude helps you think through the situation, the conversation structure, the questions to ask. None of that requires the student's name. The privacy gate protects your student and your professional standing.
beats.new_visual_element: Artifact view — anonymized prompt: same help / same preparation / no FERPA risk

beats.narration_text: Use this template for your next advising preparation session.

props.command: I have an advising appointment tomorrow. I am not sharing any identifying information. Here is the situation: [describe in terms of year / situation / course type / issue — no names, IDs, or records]. Help me: (1) think through the likely concerns this student has, (2) identify the institutional options available, (3) draft three opening questions that give the student room to lead the conversation.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Advising Privacy Gate: Anonymize Before Prompting] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
